author: @BullmooseBeerTalks
text: Mate, This is really bad advice. If you do any of these, youll be the office weirdo no one wants to hang out with. Only Follow this video's advice if you want to be sad and lonely  You should start by assuming the joke is friendly banter instead of some "red pilled" power play. Then take the comedic momentum by showing a little humility with a related self depricating followup, and finish with a quick joke back at the person.  This is a normal social situation. People make jokes, people tease, friends make fun of each other all the time. Learn to interact socially or be stressed and angry all the time.

Here's a silly notion, what if the jokes are actually funny but then you "be that guy" and you react "emotionless" asking rhetorical questions which literally only comes across as serious, doesn't THAT give more weight to the person making the joke?! Isn't the whole point to do the total opposite?! The best reaction?! No reaction! But that's boring and requires actual psychological understanding or stoicism so obviously you can't do that.

author: @EscúfoDelSombrerón
text: Another one is to play completely dumb and to say with all the innocence you could muster, "What exactly did you mean by that?" If they get suddenly shy and try to deflect, insist, "I genuinely do not understand what you mean, can you please explain?" Unless they are out to very pointedly and demonstratively take you down in public (which is highly unlikely in most corporate office settings), this should be enough to drive your point home and establish dominance in the situation.

author: @bryantgeorgen986
text: I'm a salesperson and I was following up with a prospect. The person that set up his previous system was there because he felt threatened that I might take the business away from him. I did not know he was going to be there.  when I approached my prospect the man was there with him. Not knowing who he was I started talking to him and he injected an interrupting insult at me. He was trying to intimidate me. I looked at him straight in the eye, paused for a few seconds, and said, "why don't you go outside and let the adults talk." His face turned be red! He started walking towards the door and beckoned me to come out with him. I told him, "just go play in the parking lot and I'll be out in a minute." The prospect was unimpressed with the man's behavior. When I finally left the establishment, he was nowhere to be found LOL  I'm sure you don't teach that response LOL
